theme_and_archetype.description: Premium, moody, cinematic dark mode. Eradicates the 'cheap toyish' cyan/magenta. Replaced with deep obsidian black, rich crimson accents, and soft pearl highlights derived directly from the character's outfit. The vibe is 'High-End Tech Concept'—think Apple mixed with cyberpunk, but sterile and elegant.

headings.description: High-impact geometric sans. Used for hero and massive text. Always tightly tracked.

body.description: Clean, highly legible body text. Prevents the 'generic Inter' look.

mono.description: Used for tech-specs, AI metrics, labels, overlines, and chat demo timestamps.

bento_grid.wrapper: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 md:gap-6

motion_and_interactions.global: Use Lenis for smooth momentum scrolling. All major section entries should have a slow, subtle upward fade (Framer Motion).

character_rigging.concept: Lucy physicalizes the scroll experience. She exists in a `fixed` or `sticky` pointer-events-none layer above the content (z-40).
character_rigging.execution: Use Framer Motion's `useScroll` and `useTransform` to move her vertically/horizontally as the user scrolls. Swap her sprite (``) when the scroll position hits specific section refs.

states.action: Slides left slightly to point at the bento grid.
Scales up slightly, speech bubble pops in.
Moves to the corner, secret/quiet gesture.
Waves goodbye near the final CTA.

character_rigging.speech_bubble: Glassmorphic bubble (`backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/5 border border-white/10`) attached to her component. Text inside should type out using a Typewriter effect when entering a new section.

motion_and_interactions.buttons: Hover state should trigger a subtle red glow (`shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(217,22,54,0.3)]`) and slightly lift (`-translate-y-0.5`).

glassmorphism.usage: Use for the sticky navbar, floating chat demo, and Lucy's speech bubbles.

cards.base: bg-[#0C0A0A] border border-white/5 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden relative
cards.inner_glow: Add a radial gradient in the top corner of cards to simulate a soft internal light.

character_sprites.variants: Assume the 6 pose variants (base, waving, pointing, excited, shush, thumbs-up) are available in the public/assets/ directory or follow the naming convention of the base URL. If not available yet, implement a robust swap logic using the base image temporarily.

background_textures.description: Dark abstract red/black texture. Use as a low-opacity (`opacity-20`) background blend in the Emotion Engine or Hero section to break up the solid black.

company_logos.description: Use for mocked 'Powered By' or Partner logos. Apply a `grayscale brightness-200 opacity-50` filter to make them premium white stencils.

component_instructions.Hero: Headline must use `text-white`. Include a minimalist waitlist form (`Input` + `Button`) using shadcn. The character should overlap the right side of the hero.
component_instructions.Chat_Demo: A terminal-like chat card. `bg-[#050505] border border-white/10`. Messages should have timestamps in `JetBrains Mono`. AI messages use the red accent (`text-[#D91636]`).
component_instructions.Bento_Grid: Map out 'Vision', 'TTS/STT', 'Local AI', 'Memory'. Use Lucide React icons. 'Vision' card could be large (col-span-2) with a simulated bounding-box UI inside it.
component_instructions.Sticky_Emotion_Section: Left side (`w-1/2`) is `sticky top-1/3`. Contains the heading 'Emotion Engine'. Right side scrolls through emotional states (Joy, Anger, Empathy) mapped to cards. Lucy character reacts alongside.
component_instructions.Footer: Massive text 'DEXALAB.ORG' spanning full width. Tiny copyright and links below it.

testing.attributes: All interactive elements (buttons, inputs, waitlist form, FAQ accordions) MUST include a `data-testid` attribute in kebab-case (e.g., `data-testid='hero-waitlist-submit'`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the color palette. DO NOT USE ANY CYAN OR MAGENTA.
Ensure `lucide-react` is used for premium iconography. No thick or cartoony icons.
Implement the `<LucyPresenter />` as a fixed overlay tracking scroll position via Framer Motion. This is the centerpiece. Make it flawless.
Use Tailwind's arbitrary values for precise coloring if needed, e.g., `bg-[#050505]`.
Do not build HTML inputs from scratch; use Shadcn `Input` and `Button` but restyle them to match the dark premium theme (black backgrounds, white borders, red hover states).
